turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Now we are going to change, let's practice if we are like in a bank and I want to open a bank account

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure! Is this bank account for personal use or for your business?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It's for personal use, and I have a question. What type of accounts are available for students?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright. For students, we usually offer two main types: a student everyday account with no monthly fees, and a student savings account with higher interest. Are you looking mainly for daily use, like payments and transfers, or more for saving money?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I'm interested in daily use, like payments and transfers.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. The student everyday account would be best then—it includes a debit card, online banking, and no account-keeping fees. Are you currently studying in Australia or overseas?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, I'm a student in Australia, so I need to have a bank account.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect. Do you already have any form of ID here, like your passport or student ID, to open the account?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, I wrote my passport and my student ID.Do I need something else? Do I need to make an appointment?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s great—you’ve got the main documents. You don’t need an appointment; you can just walk in. But if you prefer, we can book a time to make it quicker. Would you like to book an appointment anyway?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No, no, it's fine. I can wait.Do I need another document or just my student ID and my passport?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Those two are usually enough, but it’s helpful if you also bring proof of your address, like a rental agreement or a recent bill. Do you already have an Australian address to use for the account?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, of course I have an Australian address, but unfortunately I didn't bring proof of my address.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s okay. You can still start the process today—we’ll just need you to email or bring the proof later to finish setting up the account. Do you want your debit card sent to your address or picked up at the branch?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I prefer that you send to my address, because the bank is a little far from my house. So, please, can you send the debit card to my address?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, we can do that. It usually arrives within 5–7 business days. Would you like to set up online banking access at the same time?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, set up online banking, it could be fine. Do I need my cell phone for the set up online?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, you’ll need your phone to verify your identity and get a security code during setup. Do you already have an Australian phone number registered in your name?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, yesterday I went to the telephonic company. So, now I have...

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect! Then you’ll be able to link your number easily. Would you like to receive bank alerts and account updates by text message or only by email?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, by text message it will be ok.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, I’ll note that. Just one last thing—would you like your account to include an overdraft option, or do you prefer to spend only the money you have available?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: What is the meaning of the overdrive option?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good question! An overdraft means the bank lets you spend a bit more than what’s in your account—like a small loan—if you run out of money. You’d pay it back later with some interest. Would you like that option, or prefer to keep it simple with no overdraft?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Oh, sounds very, very interesting, but no, thank you. I prefer just spend only the money that I have in my account.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, that’s a safe choice. Everything’s ready then—you’ll get your debit card by mail and your online banking access today. Would you like me to explain how to activate your card when it arrives?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright. When your debit card arrives, just log in to your online banking app or website, enter the last four digits of your card, and follow the steps to create your PIN. It only takes a minute. Do you want me to explain how to use the card for payments, too?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No, that's not necessary. I have friends and I have seen how they use their accounts. But if in the future I have a question, maybe can I come here and ask you? Right?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course, absolutely! You can come anytime or call us if you have questions. We’re always happy to help. Anything else you’d like to check before we finish opening your account today?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yeah, one more question. Is there a monthly fee for this account?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good question. No, there’s no monthly fee for student accounts—as long as you’re enrolled as a student, it stays free. Would you like me to email you the full account details and conditions?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yay, that's a good idea. And maybe when I receive the email, I have more time to read about the conditions. But until here, you are being so clear and so kind. Your explanation, it'll help me a lot. I mean, so far everything has been clear.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s really nice to hear, thank you, Pamela. I’m glad it’s all clear for you. I’ll send that email with your account details today. Is there anything else you’d like to ask before you head off?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No, thank you. That's all.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re very welcome, Pamela. Have a great day, and good luck with your studies!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Thank you. You are so kind. I hope that if I come back in one or three months, if I have a question, I can be more clear with my English. Thank you.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s such a lovely goal, Pamela! Your English is already very good—and you’re getting clearer every time you speak. I’ll be happy to talk with you again anytime. Take care and see you soon!

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Thank you so much, take care, and see you soon.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re very welcome, Pamela. Take care and see you soon! 🌷 _______________________
